figures.description: Large full-slide map image (1017x720px, 5138KB PNG). Based on context from other slides showing study area and results, this is likely a study area map or spatial results map for the Cambridgeshire road network showing link locations, weather stations, or road features.

figures.description: Model specification diagram showing the hierarchical structure of the GLM model. Contains text boxes for 'Road-specific effect', 'Link-specific effect', 'Marginal effect of rainfall on a particular road', and 'Marginal effect of rainfall on a particular link', connected by arrow connectors. Illustrates the nested road-link structure of the econometric model.
figures.potential_use: Methodology section - Model specification figure showing the hierarchical/nested structure of the GLM approach
figures.quality_notes: Vector diagram built from PowerPoint shapes (text boxes + arrow connectors). Can be recreated at any resolution. Clean and simple layout.

figures.description: Model specification diagram for local roads analysis. Shows 'Countline effect' and 'Marginal effect of rainfall on a particular Countline' connected by arrows. Independent variables listed: Volume, Precipitation, lines (Millroad, Hillsroad etc.), link, interaction terms (lines*Precipitation) and controls.

figures.description: Work package structure diagram showing WP1-WP5 as rounded rectangles with their descriptions: WP1 (Road resilience analysis), WP2 (Demand modelling using population projections), WP3 (Climate hazard mapping), WP4 (Risk evaluation), WP5 (Testing the Framework + Multi Criteria Decision Making). figures.description: Betweenness centrality map of the Cambridgeshire road network (518x547px, 766KB PNG). Shows road network with darker shading indicating more critical roads. figures.quality_notes: PNG, 766KB, 518x547px. Moderate resolution - may need to regenerate at higher resolution for print. Color-coded map with clear legend needed.
